app.browserOnlyBackend: Browser only (npm run dev): no Rust process. Use npm run desktop for the backend.

sidebar.footerHint: Vault: PIN hash in local DB, session in memory. Provider tokens in the OS keyring. Strict CSP in production.

home.emptyDescription: The main area stays empty until you add at least one provider from Explore. Everyone keeps their own pool—you only rotate what you choose.
home.withServicesSubtitle: Pick a service to see rotatable resources (coming soon).

explore.title: Choose what you want to rotate
explore.subtitle: Add only the providers you use. Rotation is always per resource, never global.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare
cloudflare.blurb: API tokens and account permissions. Suited for guided rotation.

supabase.title: Supabase
supabase.blurb: Project keys and tenant-bound secrets. Some operations via API.

oauth_google.title: OAuth (Google)
oauth_google.blurb: OAuth client ID/secret. Often requires steps in Google Cloud Console.

services.empty: You have not connected any services yet. Add them from Explore to build your personal pool.
services.title: Personal pool
services.subtitle: Click a row to open the service detail (Cloudflare is the first supported).

placeholder.body: We integrate one service at a time. After Cloudflare, Supabase and OAuth (Google) will follow the same pattern: link account, list rotatable resources, then rotation actions.

vault.setupBody: Your PIN is never stored in plaintext—only an Argon2 hash in the app’s local database (<0>rotate.db</0>). Without the PIN you cannot access integrations or secrets.

cloudflare.intro: Management token in the OS credential store. To list and rotate tokens you need at least <0>Account · API Tokens · Read</0> and <1>Edit</1> (create and delete).
cloudflare.apiTokenPh: Saved in the keyring after verification
cloudflare.noTokens: No tokens listed (check your management token permissions).
cloudflare.footnote: Rotate creates a new token with the same policies as the selected one. The new secret is shown only once—copy it immediately or update the vault if it is Rotate’s management token.
cloudflare.confirmUnlink: Remove Account ID and token from the keyring?
cloudflare.confirmReveal: Anyone who can see your screen can read the token. Continue?
cloudflare.rotateLead: A new token will be created with the same policies as <0>{{name}}</0>.
cloudflare.rotateUpdateVault: Update the token saved in Rotate in the keyring (required if you are rotating the management token used by this app).
cloudflare.rotateRevoke: Revoke the previous token after creating the new one.
cloudflare.rotateWarn: If you revoke the management token without updating the vault, the app cannot call Cloudflare until you paste a valid token.
cloudflare.resultLead: Save this value now—Cloudflare will not show it again.
cloudflare.revealLead: Do not share your screen. Close when done.
